crawl_scope.fetch_mechanism: curl with desktop UA, all routes return identical 45,788-byte HTML shell — per-route meta + JSON-LD are baked into a single <script id="__SEO_DATA__" type="application/json"> block and swapped client-side by a pre-hydration IIFE

architecture.framework: Vite + React SPA (no SSR, no router — single index.html for every route)
architecture.seo_pattern: Static noJS shell (div#dc-seo-shell) with H1, nav, 7 H2 sections, OL+UL lists, footer — visible to crawlers/disabled-JS users, hidden when React mounts. Per-route title/description/keywords/canonical/og/twitter are stored in __SEO_DATA__ JSON and swapped by an inline IIFE that runs before module load.
architecture.per_route_handling: Every route has bespoke title, description, keywords in __SEO_DATA__. Canonical, og:url, og:title, og:description, twitter:url, twitter:title, twitter:description are all swapped per route by the IIFE. Blog posts additionally have jsonLd (BlogPosting + BreadcrumbList) injected as <script type="application/ld+json"> after hydration.

details.title: dreamclerk — career simulation platform for indian undergraduates

details.note: very short, wastes SERP real estate

h1.static_shell_h1: career simulation platform for indian undergraduates
h1.issue: The static H1 is identical for every route. The IIFE swaps title/description/keywords/canonical/og/twitter but never touches H1, H2s, or any body content of the static shell. So for /about, /faq, /tracks, /workspace, /how, all 10 blog posts — a no-JS crawler or any crawler that does not wait for hydration sees the BRAND TAGLINE as the H1, not the route H1. This is a Google 'cloaking-like' risk if Google ignores the IIFE entirely.
h1.all_routes_h1_in_react_app: likely yes (React app sets real H1 per section), but invisible to crawlers that don't execute JS / don't wait for hydration

static_shell_h2_topics: the workspace — what you ship with
h2_h3_hierarchy.h3_gap: no H3 in static shell, no sub-topic hierarchy for the SPA fallback

canonical.static_shell_canonical: https://www.dreamclerk.com/ (hardcoded — IIFE swaps per route after hydration)

open_graph.og:title: per-route via IIFE
open_graph.og:image:alt: per page (homepage: 'dreamclerk — career simulation platform hero with terminal aesthetic')

examples: the 90-second internship interview that changed 14% of outcomes

word_count_per_page.blog_posts_actual_content: hidden inside React app — not in the static shell; crawlers that don't wait for hydration see only the static shell
word_count_per_page.issue: For blog posts, the static shell shows only the homepage 483 words, not the article body. A 1,200-word blog post on fresher unemployment in India becomes a 483-word snippet to Googlebot — catastrophic for blog SEO unless Googlebot reliably executes the IIFE + waits for hydration.

keyword_signals.primary_keywords_per_route: well-targeted per page (career simulation platform, in-browser ide, AI code reviewer, fresher unemployment, coding interview with no experience, etc.)

content_freshness.modified_at_dates: all present and distinct from published_at where relevant
content_freshness.recency_signal: STRONG — blog posts dated within the last 2 months, lastmod on sitemap is 2026-06-16, posts are clearly clustered as a content series

schema_validity.syntactic: all 24 JSON-LD blocks parse cleanly as valid JSON

completeness.Organization: complete (name, logo, founder, areaServed, address, contactPoint, sameAs with 5 social profiles, foundingDate 2025)
completeness.SoftwareApplication: complete with featureList, offers (PreOrder, INR 0), aggregateRating, 3 reviews
completeness.BlogPosting: complete (headline, description, author, publisher, datePublished, dateModified, mainEntityOfPage, image, keywords, articleSection, inLanguage en-IN)

schema_issues: SoftwareApplication review dates (2026-02-22, 2026-03-08, 2026-04-12) precede some blog dates — fine

AggregateRating 4.9/1847 with only 3 visible Review items is implausible (ratio 615:1); Google's rich-results policy requires that aggregateRating count match self-collected reviews — risk of manual action if scaled
No 'aggregateRating' reviewed-by linkage to the 3 named Review objects — schema-level consistency gap
Author on BlogPosting is an Organization ('dreamclerk team'), not a Person — fine for corporate blog but reduces E-E-A-T person signal
Person schema is only used for the 3 anonymous Review authors in SoftwareApplication — no Person schema for founders, team, or named contributors

robots_txt.note: feed.xml referenced in robots but not crawled; recommend also adding llms.txt reference for AI crawlers

sitemap_xml.lastmod: all 2026-06-16 (uniform, not per-page) — loses freshness signal at URL granularity
sitemap_xml.changefreq: weekly for home + blog, monthly for core, yearly for blog posts + legal — defensible
sitemap_xml.missing: no image sitemap, no news sitemap (despite the news namespace declaration), no video sitemap

llms_txt.format: matches llms.txt spec (markdown, H1, sections with link lists, blockquote summary)
llms_txt.freshness: 2025 footer year — likely outdated vs 2026 publish dates
llms_txt.llms_full_txt: PRESENT but is BYTE-IDENTICAL to llms.txt — the 'full' version should be much richer (full prose of each page); this is a missed GEO opportunity

author_information.author_credential_signal: low — all blog posts attribute to 'dreamclerk team' (Organization), no Person entity, no bio, no LinkedIn, no Twitter handles for individual authors
author_information.byline_on_pages: absent in static shell (would need React hydration to render)

about_page.title: about — the team behind dreamclerk
about_page.description_meta: founded in chennai in 2025 by ananya, raghav, and priya. bootstrapped. never charging students what they can't pay. the team page, the advisors, the press, and the funding story. (178c — over 165c cap)

trust_signals.testimonials_present_in_app: yes (3 in static JSON-LD Reviews, named: aanya sharma, karthik r, mira patil)
trust_signals.press: mentioned in /about description ('the press, and the funding story') but not in static shell

organization_schema.name: dreamclerk
organization_schema.issue: founder should be 3 Persons (ananya, raghav, priya), not a single generic 'team' Person

specific_numbers_in_copy: r=0.12, r=0.40, r=0.38, r=0.34 (resume vs 3 signals)
factual_density.data_driven: strong — multiple cohort outcome statistics, real numbers, real effect sizes
factual_density.named_studies_or_citations: none external — all numbers are dreamclerk's own (cohort data); defensible for an LLM citation but lacks external anchoring

content_for_ai_synthesis.external_source_citations: low — no links to BLS, NSSO, McKinsey, NASSCOM, or any external authoritative source backing the 73%/85% statistics
content_for_ai_synthesis.comprehensiveness: high — FAQPage covers 8 Qs, blog covers 10 topics, /about + /workspace + /tracks + /companies all distinct surfaces

entity_clarity.people: aanya, raghav, priya, aanya sharma, karthik r, mira patil, dreamclerk team — partially inconsistent (aanya appears in both founder list and review list without disambiguation)

originality_signals.own_data: yes (cohort 1/cohort 2 numbers, rubric changes, in-browser IDE architecture post-mortem)
originality_signals.point_of_view: strong — 'we changed 14% of outcomes', 'we got wrong', 'the resume is dead'

structured_data_depth.Organization: complete with founder, address, contact, sameAs

clean_crawlability.no_canonical_mismatch_in_data: all 20 routes have self-referencing canonical via IIFE

technical_geo.llms_full_txt_presence: present but duplicated — not the differentiated 'full' content it should be

featured_snippet_eligibility.direct_answer_paragraphs_below_questions: partial — FAQ page (per the noJS shell) lists 19 questions but the static shell shows them as a list, not in Q-then-A-40-60-words format
featured_snippet_eligibility.definition_patterns: present (FAQ Q1 'what is dreamclerk?' followed by 49-word answer; 'what is a career simulation platform?')
featured_snippet_eligibility.list_content: excellent — 8-step how-it-works list (numbered OL), 6 tracks (UL), 6 companies (UL), 8 features (UL)
featured_snippet_eligibility.table_content: absent in static shell (Workspace section would benefit from a feature comparison table)

questions: what is a career simulation platform?
faq_schema.correctness: syntactically valid, all questions have acceptedAnswer.text
faq_schema.issue: FAQ page description says '19 questions' but static FAQPage JSON-LD only has 8 — drift between UI count and structured data

structured_answer_formats.howto_schema: ABSENT — /how page describes 8 steps in an OL but no HowTo JSON-LD emitted; a 8-step process described in plain prose is prime HowTo territory and missed
structured_answer_formats.question_phrased_h2_h3: the static shell uses STATEMENTS (e.g. 'how it works — 8 steps', 'the workspace — what you ship with') rather than QUESTION H2s (e.g. 'How does dreamclerk work?') — limits direct-question snippet eligibility

voice_search_readiness.conversational_language: high — copy is short, declarative, sentence-case, no jargon
voice_search_readiness.long_tail_question_coverage: blog titles ARE long-tail questions (e.g. 'how to get hired as a fresher with no internship and no network', 'how to pass a coding interview with no experience') — STRONG

local_signals.nap_consistency: Chennai, India declared in Organization schema; no GBP profile linked
local_signals.local_pack_eligibility: n/a — SaaS, not a local business

seo.rationale: The Vite SPA shell with pre-hydration IIFE is a well-engineered approach for an SPA. Bespoke per-route titles/descriptions/keywords/canonical/OG/Twitter for all 20 routes is above average. JSON-LD coverage is broad (4 static + 2 per blog post). Robots.txt, sitemap.xml, lastmod cadence are clean. The big losses: (1) static shell H1 never updates per route (so no-JS crawlers see brand tagline on every URL), (2) 4 of 20 titles over 60c, 9 of 20 descriptions over 165c, (3) llms-full.txt is byte-identical to llms.txt (missed opportunity), (4) the static shell content is only 483 words, so blog posts appear thin to non-hydrating crawlers, (5) uniform lastmod on sitemap hides per-page freshness, (6) AggregateRating count mismatch (1847 vs 3 reviews visible).

geo.rationale: E-E-A-T is the weak point: 'dreamclerk team' is the only author entity (no Person for the 3 named founders), the in-shell about-page teaser mentions ananya/raghav/priya but never promotes them to schema. Originality signals are strong (own cohort data, named statistics, original rubric changes). llms.txt exists (rare). Missing: ClaimReview on the bias-audit post, Dataset schema for the cohort numbers, SpeakableSpecification, more Person entities. Contact info is email-only, no phone, no physical street — OK for SaaS but reduces local trust. SameAs links to 5 social profiles is good.

aeo.rationale: FAQPage JSON-LD is present (8 Qs) but the FAQ page actually contains 19 questions (drift). The /how page is begging for HowTo schema and doesn't have it. Question-phrased blog titles are AEO gold and they execute that well. Voice-readiness is strong via short declarative copy. SpeakableSpecification is missing entirely. The workspace section's 8 features (Monaco, terminal, Docker, Jupyter, SQL, AI reviewer, PR, git) is a perfect 8-cell comparison table opportunity that isn't taken.

E_top_priorities.title: Make the static shell H1 per-route (today every URL shows 'career simulation platform for indian undergraduates')
E_top_priorities.why: The IIFE swaps title/description/keywords/canonical/og/twitter but never touches the H1, H2s, or body content of the static shell. A crawler that doesn't execute the pre-hydration script (or executes it but doesn't wait long enough) sees the brand tagline as the H1 for /about, /tracks, /workspace, /faq, and all 10 blog posts. Either (a) extend the IIFE to swap the H1 per route, or (b) ship a per-route prerender so the static H1 matches the React H1.
E_top_priorities.impact: highest — affects all 20 routes' semantic clarity for non-hydrating crawlers

E_top_priorities.title: Tighten title + description lengths to SERP caps (50-60c / 150-160c)
E_top_priorities.why: 5 blog titles are 72-87 chars (will truncate in SERP), 1 home title is 65c, /faq /about /privacy /terms titles are 34-43c (wasting SERP real estate). 9 of 20 descriptions exceed 165c — Google's snippet will rewrite them.
E_top_priorities.impact: high — every truncated title loses CTR, every overwritten meta description loses message control

E_top_priorities.title: Differentiate llms-full.txt from llms.txt (currently byte-identical)
E_top_priorities.why: llms-full.txt is the GEO deliverable LLMs are most likely to ingest. It should contain full prose of /how, /workspace, /tracks, /companies, /faq, /about, plus the body of all 10 blog posts. Right now it's 18 lines — same as llms.txt. The 'full' suffix is a promise the file doesn't keep.
E_top_priorities.impact: high for GEO — Perplexity, ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini all consult llms-full.txt first when present

E_top_priorities.title: Promote the 3 founders (ananya, raghav, priya) to Person schema + per-author bylines on blog posts
E_top_priorities.why: E-E-A-T for a YC-style founder story hinges on Person entities. Right now Organization.founder is a generic Person('dreamclerk team') and all 10 blog posts credit 'dreamclerk team'. Adding 3 Person entities (with sameAs to LinkedIn, bios on /about, photo URLs) plus rotating bylines per post will materially lift GEO trust.
E_top_priorities.impact: high for E-E-A-T scoring, moderate for AI citations

E_top_priorities.title: Add HowTo schema on /how (8-step process is already an OL) and SpeakableSpecification on FAQPage
E_top_priorities.why: /how is a perfect HowTo (8 ordered steps with description + tool + result). FAQPage is a perfect Speakable (8 short Q+A pairs that read well aloud). Both are <2 hours of work and unlock HowTo rich results + voice/AI read-aloud eligibility.
E_top_priorities.impact: medium-high — AEO rich-result eligibility, voice assistant citation, AI-overview inclusion

F_strengths.evidence: All 20 routes have bespoke title, description, keywords, canonical, og, twitter in __SEO_DATA__ with an inline IIFE that swaps them pre-hydration. No template-generated garbage.
Blog posts ship full BlogPosting + BreadcrumbList JSON-LD (10 of 10), with valid datePublished, dateModified, publisher logo, inLanguage en-IN, and 3-step breadcrumb.
Robots.txt, sitemap.xml, llms.txt, llms-full.txt all exist and are well-formed. Sitemap has correct priority distribution and all 20 URLs.

Factual density is high and original: '1,200 applicants', '14% callback', 'r=0.12 vs r=0.40', '73% unemployed', '85% of JDs'. These are LLM-citation-ready statistics — far better than the average startup site.
Static noJS shell is unusually rich: 1 H1, 7 H2, 2 nav, 1 OL, 5 UL, 35 internal links with descriptive anchor text, footer. This is what Googlebot sees first and it reads well.
